stem: In a figure, lines j and k are cut by a transversal. One angle formed at line j has measure 55 degrees. At line k, the four angles formed by the transversal have measures p, q, r, and s degrees, where p corresponds to the 55-degree angle, q is supplementary to p, r is vertical to p, and s is vertical to q. Which of the following equations alone is sufficient to prove that lines j and k are parallel?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. Two lines cut by a transversal are parallel if and only if a pair of corresponding angles are congruent. The angle of measure p degrees corresponds to the given 55-degree angle, so p = 55 forces the lines to be parallel.

Choice A is incorrect: q is supplementary to p, so q = 55 would force p = 125.

Choice B is incorrect: s is vertical to q, not corresponding to the 55-degree angle.

Choice D is incorrect: q = 35 does not match the supplement of a 55-degree corresponding angle.
